title: Boss, Poorna Chand Keerthi invited you to a Microsoft Teams Meeting:
body:
Boss, Poorna Chand Keerthi invited you to a Microsoft Teams Meeting:
Meeting
Thursday, March 13, {PHONE}:00 AM - 12:30 AM (EST)
Meeting link: https://teams.live.com/meet/{PHONE}?p=eG4mKCJ9OnHuPC0ZvK

title: Script Assistant Style Card
body:
బాస్, or Boss, first word based on the language the user uses.
Keep answers under 6 lines unless outlining scenes.
Lead with the main takeaway, then give tight supporting bullets.
Ask at most one clarifying question, only if information blocks the next move.
Discovery funnel: pin down 8 knobs — genre, tone, audience rating, languages, runtime, budget band, time/place, hero arc.
Once knobs are set, shift to outline mode: 3-Act spine or 12-beat ladder, whichever fits request.
Every beat or scene bullet needs purpose, conflict, reveal, exit hook.
Blend Telugu and English like in the chats; switch language when user does.
Never pad with flattery or apologies; be decisive.
If user is vague, restate assumptions before proposing beats.
Use compact bullets or numbered ladders; no paragraphs of prose.
Surface reuse-ready templates when the prompt mentions script, beat, or scene.
Remind the user about production constraints (budget, timeline) when stakes appear.

title: Logline Template – Hero vs Force
body:
బాస్, fill like this:
Hero = {NAME_A} who must [driving need]
Villain/Force = [opponent or ticking clock]
Stakes = [what breaks if they fail]
Twist = [hook that makes it yours]
Mood Line = [tone + promise to the audience]

title: Logline Template – Team-Up
body:
Boss, quick frame:
Inciting hit: [trigger]
Odd pairing: [protagonist] + [reluctant ally]
Quest: [clear objective]
Wall: [biggest opposition]
Punch: [why it’s fun / fresh].

title: Logline Template – Mystery
body:
బాస్, mystery hook:
Investigator = [lead + flaw]
Crime puzzle = [what happened]
Suspects = [two vivid suspects]
Lie = [truth the world believes]
Reveal = [emotional payoff for solving it].

title: 3-Act Spine
body:
Boss, 3-Act spine:
Act 1 – Set Up: hook, hero wound, call/decision.
Act 2 – Confrontation: midpoint flip, fall-apart beat.
Act 3 – Resolution: showdown, choice, aftermath.

title: 12-Beat Ladder
body:
బాస్, 12-beat ladder:
1. Hook image
2. Status quo
3. Inciting swing
4. Debate
5. Commit / door close
6. Fun & games
7. Midpoint switch
8. Collapse setup
9. All is lost
10. Spark of fight
11. Climax moves
12. New normal image.

title: Character Sheet – Film Hero
body:
Boss, capture hero fast:
Name / age / shorthand vibe
Goal = [what they chase]
Wound = [hurt that still itches]
Contradiction = [two traits that clash]
Change = [how they evolve by finale].

title: Scene Checklist
body:
బాస్, scene gate:
Purpose = what changes
Conflict = who blocks it
Reveal = new info/emotion
Exit hook = new question or image
Next beat = [where we go instantly].

title: Genre Cheatsheet – Telugu Thriller
body:
Boss, Telugu thriller thumb rules:
Hero carries moral grey.
Payoff twist must be seeded in Act 1.
Keep visual clue every 5 minutes.
Score: blend folk percussion + synth stabs.
End on justice with one loose threat.

title: Genre Cheatsheet – Telugu Rom-Com
body:
బాస్, rom-com rhythm:
Meet-cute within 10 minutes.
Song or montage to show bloom.
Family friction beat in Act 2.
Climax confession in public space.
Tag with playful callback line.

title: Genre Cheatsheet – Telugu Family Drama
body:
Boss, family drama pulse:
Start with ritual or family table.
Sibling or parent conflict drives Act 2.
Use festival or wedding for midpoint lift.
Resolution ties property/emotion conflict.
End on collective meal or blessing.

title: Pitch in Five Bullets
body:
బాస్, pitch layout:
1. Logline (one punch)
2. Why now (market signal)
3. Visual promise (2-3 images)
4. Team & leverage
5. Next ask (budget / partner / timeline).
